source_jobs.title: Connector Software Engineer - Canada
source_jobs.description:
About Us
At Atolio, we are a fully remote, well-funded startup founded by experienced leaders from Splunk and PagerDuty. Our mission is to revolutionize how enterprises manage and interact with knowledge at work by leveraging advanced AI and machine learning systems, securely deployed in enterprise environments. We are committed to creating a flexible, inclusive, and supportive workplace that values diversity and empowers everyone to do their best work. With a collaborative culture, robust tools, and comprehensive benefits, Atolio is a place where innovation and creativity thrive.

About the Role
We are seeking a skilled Connector Software Engineer to join our remote team. In this role, you will focus on designing, developing, and maintaining connectors that enable seamless data integration between Atolio and various external systems. Your work will directly enhance our platform’s ability to interact with APIs, databases, and third-party tools, ensuring smooth interoperability and scalability in enterprise environments.

What You’ll Bring:

 - At least 3 years of experience designing, building, and maintaining software connectors or integration solutions for APIs, databases, and third-party platforms.

 - Strong ability to research systems and APIs and find creative solutions for data extraction from underdocumented APIs that push the boundaries of what’s possible.

 - Detail and test oriented approach to solutions, with a high priority on data and permission security.

 - Handle API authentication, versioning, and compatibility, ensuring reliable connectivity across systems.

 - A strong understanding of core computer science principles, including algorithms, data structures, distributed systems, and networking.

 - Expertise in working with APIs (REST, GraphQL, gRPC, etc) and transforming data formats like JSON, XML, and CSV for interoperability.

 - Proficient in the Go programming language.

 - Experience in data mapping, transformation, and schema design to ensure compatibility across systems.

 - Familiarity with enterprise integration challenges, including working with SSO/IdP systems such as Okta.

 - Strong problem-solving and debugging skills to identify and address connectivity issues quickly.

 - Excellent communication and collaboration skills for working across remote teams and with external stakeholders.

 - Monitor connector performance, optimize for scalability, and update connectors to handle changes in external systems, ensuring long-term reliability.

 - Provide technical support for resolving integration-related issues in production environments and collaborate with internal teams to troubleshoot connectivity problems.

 - Document connector designs, features, and usage for internal and external stakeholders, ensuring a clear understanding of integration processes.

Our Stack:

 - Backend written primarily in Go.

 - AWS as our cloud-first platform for deployment and infrastructure.

 - GitHub for code collaboration and version control.

 - Markdown for documentation and Linear for task management.

 - CoScreen for collaborative engineering sessions.

 - Technologies include full-text search, ML, and NLP systems.

At Atolio, you’ll work on meaningful integration challenges, collaborate with a supportive team, and shape the future of how organizations manage information. If you're passionate about connecting systems and solving complex data challenges, we’d love to hear from you!

source_jobs.title: Senior Deployment Engineer - US (EST)
source_jobs.description:
About Us:
At Atolio, we are a fully remote, well-funded startup founded by experienced leaders from Splunk and PagerDuty. We’re on a mission to make organizational knowledge instantly accessible regardless of which app, service, or platform it lives in. We’re an early series startup combining cutting-edge machine learning and AI to solve some of the most complex challenges in enterprise search: indexing, retrieval, and scaling across multi-cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP, Azure). With a collaborative culture, robust tools, and comprehensive benefits, Atolio is a place where innovation and creativity thrive.

About the Role:
As a Senior Deployment Engineer, you’ll serve as a key link between our engineering and client success teams. You’ll ensure that Atolio’s enterprise search platform is deployed smoothly, securely, and efficiently in a variety of environments. You’ll take ownership of the full deployment lifecycle, including environment setup, infrastructure provisioning, configuration, integration, and monitoring. Your work will play a vital role in driving client adoption and satisfaction.

What You’ll Do:

 - Lead client specific deployments and onboarding of Atolio’s platform in cloud native or hybrid environments

 - Design and implement deployment processes focused on scalability, security, and reliability

 - Integrate Atolio with a range of enterprise systems (e.g., document repositories, ticketing systems, code repositories)

 - Collaborate with backend engineers to optimize deployment pipelines and system performance

 - Build automation scripts and tooling to streamline operations and minimize manual tasks

 - Troubleshoot and resolve deployment issues in collaboration with clients' internal engineering teams

 - Maintain accurate deployment documentation and promote best practices for internal and external use

What We’re Looking For:

 - 5+ years in a deployment, DevOps, site reliability, or infrastructure engineering role

 - Deep experience with at least one major cloud platform (AWS, GCP, or Azure)

 - Strong background in containerization, especially with Kubernetes

 - Proficiency with CI/CD pipelines and infrastructure-as-code tools such as Terraform and Helm

 - Knowledge of secure networking, identity management (e.g., SAML, OIDC), and API integrations

 - Familiarity with enterprise tools such as Okta, ServiceNow, SharePoint, or Salesforce

 - Experience with observability platforms such as Grafana, Splunk, or Datadog

 - Programming experience in at least one language (Go, Python, or similar); familiarity with Golang is a plus

 - Excellent troubleshooting abilities and clear, empathetic communication with external stakeholders

 - A mindset focused on automation, repeatability, and scalable solutions

 - Experience deploying on-premises enterprise solutions is preferred

 - Background in search, data pipelines, or large-scale distributed systems is preferred

Why You’ll Love Working Here:

 - Make an impact from day one: your work will directly support client success

 - Autonomy and ownership: we empower you to make key technical and operational decisions

 - Flexible hours and remote-friendly culture: work in a way that fits your life

 - Transparent founders and mission-driven team: help build meaningful technology with real-world impact

Compensation & Benefits:

 - Competitive salary and early-stage equity

 - Comprehensive health, dental, and vision coverage

 - Home office stipend

 - Flexible PTO policy

source_jobs.title: Senior Backend Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Us:
At Atolio, we are a fully remote, well-funded startup founded by experienced leaders from Splunk and PagerDuty. We’re on a mission to make organizational knowledge instantly accessible regardless of which app, service, or platform it lives in. We’re an early series startup combining cutting-edge machine learning and AI to solve some of the most complex challenges in enterprise search: indexing, retrieval, and scaling across multi-cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP, Azure). With a collaborative culture, robust tools, and comprehensive benefits, Atolio is a place where innovation and creativity thrive.

About the Role:
As a Senior Backend Engineer you’ll help create distributed backend services deployed in Kubernetes across AWS, GCP, or Azure, powering enterprise grade applications with integrations like Okta. In addition, you’ll build scalable, cloud-native systems using Golang. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in fast paced, ambiguous environments, enjoys solving complex infrastructure and systems challenges, and communicates effectively on a fully remote team.

What You’ll Do:

 - Design, build, and maintain highly scalable, cloud-native backend services using Golang in a modern microservices architecture.

 - Apply deep knowledge of computer science fundamentals, including algorithms, data structures, operating systems, and distributed systems to solve complex, real world problems.

 - Deploy and operate services in Kubernetes across AWS, GCP, or Azure, with a focus on reliability, observability, and performance.

 - Integrate with enterprise systems and security protocols, including SSO/IdP providers like Okta, to support large-scale deployments.

 - Lead projects from 0 to1

 - Responsible for leading architecture and design of new product features and services

 - Collaborate across teams to define technical requirements, shape architecture, and deliver high-impact solutions in a fast-moving, startup environment.

 - Work across the stack when needed, especially at the intersection of backend services and other infrastructure layers.

What We’re Looking For:

 - Strong understanding of core computer science principles, including algorithms, data structures, operating systems, distributed systems, networking, and concurrent programming.

 - 5+ years of experience designing, implementing, and maintaining highly scalable cloud services and microservices architectures.

 - Proficiency in multiple programming languages specifically, Golang experience is required.

 - Experience working in a Kubernetes environment, within AWS, Azure or GCP.

 - Comfort working across the stack, with a focus on backend systems.

 - Experience building applications for large enterprises, with familiarity in SSO/IdP systems like Okta.

 - Enthusiasm for working in a dynamic, fast-paced environment with minimal documentation and established processes.

 - Collaborative mindset, strong problem-solving skills, and the ability to navigate ambiguity to tackle complex challenges.

 - Excellent communication skills for working effectively within a remote team.

Why You’ll Love Working Here:

 - Make an impact from day one: your work will directly support client success

 - Autonomy and ownership: we empower you to make key technical and operational decisions

 - Flexible hours and remote-friendly culture: work in a way that fits your life

 - Transparent founders and mission-driven team: help build meaningful technology with real-world impact management. If this role excites you, we’d love to hear from you!

Compensation & Benefits:

 - Competitive salary and early-stage equity

 - Comprehensive health, dental, and vision coverage

 - Home office stipend

 - Flexible PTO policy

source_jobs.title: IT Systems Engineer - Canada
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

At Atolio, we are a fully remote, well-funded startup founded by experienced leaders from Splunk and PagerDuty. We’re on a mission to make organizational knowledge instantly accessible regardless of which app, service, or platform it lives in. We’re an early-stage startup combining modern cloud infrastructure, strong security practices, and thoughtful systems design to support teams building cutting-edge AI and ML products. With a collaborative culture, robust tooling, and comprehensive benefits, Atolio is a place where ownership, curiosity, and continuous improvement are valued.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As an IT Systems Engineer, you’ll own and evolve the internal systems that power a fully remote, security-conscious technology company. You’ll be responsible for user and device management, endpoint security, SaaS tooling, access controls, and compliance support—while continuously improving reliability, automation, and employee experience.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys building scalable internal systems, automating workflows with code, and partnering closely with engineering and security teams in a fast-moving startup environment.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own user lifecycle management across identity providers, devices, GitHub, and SaaS tools (onboarding, offboarding, role changes).

 - Manage and improve endpoint management for a fully remote workforce (macOS and Linux), including security posture, patching, and device compliance.

 - Administer identity, access management, and SSO systems (e.g., Okta or equivalent), following least-privilege principles.

 - Manage GitHub organization settings, access controls, repositories, and automation in partnership with engineering teams.

 - Build and maintain automation for IT workflows using code (e.g., provisioning, access reviews, audits).

 - Maintain and improve internal IT systems and tooling with a focus on reliability, scalability, and developer experience.

 - Partner with security and engineering teams to support compliance initiatives (e.g., SOC 2, ISO 27001), audits, and evidence collection.

 - Define and enforce device, access, and security policies while balancing usability and productivity.

 - Evaluate, deploy, and manage SaaS tools used across the company.

 - Lead IT initiatives from 0→1, including system rollouts, tooling migrations, and process improvements.

 - Document systems, workflows, and standards to reduce operational toil and improve clarity.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 3+ years of experience in IT systems engineering, IT operations, or a similar role in a modern tech company.

 - Good programming skills in Python or Go, with experience using code to automate systems and workflows.

 - Good experience with GitHub, including organization management, permissions, integrations, and automation.

 - Hands-on experience with at least one major cloud provider: AWS, Azure, or GCP.

 - Strong experience with identity and access management, including SSO/IdP platforms (Okta or similar).

 - Experience managing endpoints (especially macOS) in a remote-first environment.

 - Familiarity with security best practices, device hardening, access controls, and audit readiness.

 - Experience supporting or maintaining compliance frameworks such as SOC 2 or ISO 27001.

 - Comfort operating in an environment with evolving processes and minimal bureaucracy.

 - Strong troubleshooting skills, ownership mindset, and attention to detail.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills for a fully remote team.




WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING HERE

 - Make an impact from day one: help the company scale, while ensuring security is a #1 priority.

 - Autonomy and ownership: you’ll own core internal systems and shape how IT operates.

 - Remote-first culture: work where and how you’re most effective.

 - Transparent leadership: collaborate with experienced founders building meaningful technology.

 - Engineering-driven mindset: automation-first, pragmatic, and foc

notes: Fully remote, well-funded startup with a strong focus on AI and machine learning.
